title: Close assess quality gates and determinism proof
description: Close the remaining assess quality gates after integration, proving determinism and contract completeness without expanding the product surface.
acceptance_criteria:
- The named assess test matrix from the plan is complete and passing, including refusal coverage, schema validation, determinism, witness coverage, and pack compatibility.
- CI and release workflows are present and consistent with the implemented repo state.
- Final quality gates run cleanly with no remaining contract drift between plan, schemas, operator metadata, and implementation.
- Any leftover non-blocking follow-up is captured as new beads before closing this lane.
notes: Primary files: remaining test files/golden fixtures and any tiny gate-fix touches needed to close drift. This is not a license to do broad refactors; fix only what the gates expose.

title: Implement artifact bundle parsing and canonical tool derivation
description: Parse report artifacts into the assess bundle model, derive canonical tool identities, and build the full epistemic basis without performing policy evaluation.
acceptance_criteria:
- Artifact JSON files load into the narrowed assess basis model without inventing semantics beyond the plan contract.
- Canonical tool identity uses explicit top-level `tool` first and version-derived fallback second, with duplicate canonical tools rejected deterministically.
- Every provided artifact appears in the epistemic basis, even if not later required by policy evaluation.
- Legacy version-only fixtures and explicit-tool fixtures both resolve to the expected canonical identities in tests.
notes: Primary files: `src/bundle/**` and `tests/bundle_construct.rs`. Keep completeness checks and rule matching out of this lane.
close_reason: Implemented bundle loading, canonical tool derivation, and duplicate/bad-artifact detection with focused bundle tests.

title: Bootstrap assess workspace, crate, schemas, and swarm-safe file skeleton
description: Create the Cargo crate, module/file skeleton, schema paths, fixture paths, and test file layout that match docs/PLAN_ASSESS.md. Compileable shell only; no real decision semantics yet.
acceptance_criteria:
- Root Cargo crate compiles with a thin main/lib split and the module tree from PLAN_ASSESS.md
- schemas/, fixtures/, tests/, rules/, and operator.json paths exist at the planned locations
- The scaffold exposes compileable shell behavior only; no real assessment semantics are implemented yet
- cargo check, cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test pass on the scaffold
notes: Primary files: Cargo.toml, src/main.rs, src/lib.rs, src/cli/**, placeholder module files, schemas/**, fixtures/**, tests/**, operator.json. Reserve exact files only.
close_reason: Scaffolded assess crate, module tree, schemas, fixtures, rules, and named test layout; quality gate passed

title: Integrate end-to-end assess command path and pack-compatible artifact output
description: Integrate the completed assess subsystems into the final command path and prove the emitted JSON artifact works cleanly with pack-style downstream handling.
acceptance_criteria:
- The final `run()` path wires CLI selection, policy loading, bundle construction, evaluation, rendering, and optional witness append exactly once.
- `--json` output is accepted as a pack-compatible assess artifact in end-to-end tests.
- End-to-end tests cover representative PROCEED, PROCEED_WITH_RISK, ESCALATE, BLOCK, and refusal flows through the real command path.
- This lane is orchestration only: it composes completed subsystems rather than re-implementing their logic.
notes: Primary files: `src/lib.rs`, any minimal top-level orchestration glue, and `tests/e2e_pipeline.rs`. Avoid reopening deep feature-module ownership unless a tiny integration hook is necessary.

title: Seed shared assess fixture corpus
description: Seed the shared assess fixture files needed by policy, bundle, evaluation, rendering, and integration tests, without owning the reusable helper layer.
acceptance_criteria:
- `fixtures/policies/**`, `fixtures/artifacts/**`, and `fixtures/golden/**` contain the minimal named corpus required by the plan for PROCEED, PROCEED_WITH_RISK, ESCALATE, BLOCK, and refusal cases.
- Fixture filenames are stable and reusable across policy, bundle, evaluator, renderer, and e2e lanes.
- Golden fixtures needed by rendering/integration are seeded here, even if later beads update their exact bytes.
- This lane does not own shared test helper APIs or feature-specific assertions.
notes: Primary files: `fixtures/**` only. Leave `tests/**` helper logic and feature-specific test files to other beads.

title: Implement ordered rule matcher and decision evaluator
description: Implement the ordered assess rule matcher and decision evaluator over a complete epistemic basis, keeping all scoring/decision semantics centralized in one place.
acceptance_criteria:
- `requires` completeness is checked before rule matching and yields `E_INCOMPLETE_BASIS` when policy prerequisites are missing.
- Rules are matched in declaration order with exact equality over the narrowed v0 match surface; first match wins and missing-match-without-default yields `E_MISSING_RULE`.
- The winning rule's `decision_band`, `risk_code`, and `name` are copied exactly into the output model with no implicit promotion or demotion.
- Tests cover all four decision bands plus the key refusal paths owned by evaluation.
notes: Primary files: `src/evaluate/**` and `tests/evaluate_rules.rs`. Do not absorb JSON/human rendering concerns here beyond the shared decision model.

title: Build shared assess test-support harness
description: Build reusable test-support helpers for assess without owning the shared fixture corpus or the substantive assertions in feature-specific suites.
acceptance_criteria:
- Shared helper utilities exist for loading fixtures, normalizing paths, temporary workspace setup, and common JSON/human assertions used across later test suites.
- Helper APIs are narrow enough that feature lanes can adopt them without forcing broad rewrites.
- This lane does not own the shared fixture corpus itself or the substantive assertions inside feature-specific suites.
- The helper layer stays optional ergonomics infrastructure; it must not become a hard blocker for core semantics unless a dependency explicitly says so.
notes: Primary files: `tests/support/**` (or equivalent helper files only). Do not take ownership of `fixtures/**` or feature-specific test suites.
close_reason: Added reusable assess test-support helpers for fixtures, schemas, temp workspaces, and JSON/human assertions.

title: Add assess CI workflow and release prerequisites
description: Add the standard CI lane and release prerequisites so assess can later adopt the same release surface as the stronger spine tools.
acceptance_criteria:
- A standard Rust CI workflow runs the repo quality gate (`cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test`) plus the UBS gate.
- Release prerequisites exist and are wired correctly: `LICENSE`, `.gitignore` expectations, and `scripts/ubs_gate.sh` if required by the workflow pattern.
- CI configuration matches the proven spine-tool pattern closely enough that the release workflow can build on it without repo-specific surprises.
- The lane stops at CI/release prerequisites; it does not publish artifacts yet.
notes: Primary files: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `scripts/ubs_gate.sh`, `LICENSE`, `.gitignore`, and any minimal README note strictly required by CI/release conventions.
close_reason: CI workflow, UBS gate script, and license landed; remaining clippy failure is in active policy-loader lane

title: Add assess operator manifest and spine-rules golden-rule enforcement
description: Add the operator metadata and golden-rule enforcement artifacts that lock the assess CLI/output contract into the wider spine discipline.
acceptance_criteria:
- `operator.json` accurately describes the assess binary, command surface, exit codes, refusals, and upstream/downstream tool relationships.
- Golden-rule files cover exit-code range, no-HashMap output discipline, and witness append expectations, with reproducible tests under `cargo test`.
- The operator/golden-rule surface reflects the locked v0 contract rather than inventing new assess semantics.
- This lane owns metadata/compliance artifacts only; it does not become a second integration path.
notes: Primary files: `operator.json`, `rules/*.yml`, and `tests/golden_rules.rs`. Avoid broad edits across feature modules.

title: Implement assess v0 decision primitive
description: Tracker epic for the full assess v0 implementation graph.
acceptance_criteria:
- Epic remains a tracker only and is never the task an agent claims for direct implementation.
- Child beads together cover scaffold, schemas, fixtures, CLI, policy, bundle, evaluation, rendering, witness, operator compliance, CI, release, integration, gates, and docs.
notes: Tracker only. Use `br ready` to claim real work from child beads.
close_reason: All assess child beads are closed; docs, CI/release, quality gates, runtime, and tests are complete

title: Add assess README and AGENTS aligned to the implemented repo
description: Write final repo-local documentation once the assess workspace, workflows, and command surface are real enough to document without guesswork.
acceptance_criteria:
- `README.md` and `AGENTS.md` describe the implemented crate layout, CLI surface, schemas, witness behavior, Beads workflow, Agent Mail guidance, and release/CI commands accurately.
- Repo-local coordination rules are self-sufficient and do not require agents to infer critical workflow from the monorepo instructions.
- The docs point to `docs/PLAN_ASSESS.md` as the governing spec and align with the shipped release surface.
- This lane touches only repo-local docs unless a tiny linked metadata fix is required.

title: Render assess.v0 JSON and human output
description: Render deterministic JSON and human assess outputs from the completed decision model without re-implementing evaluation logic.
acceptance_criteria:
- JSON output serializes a deterministic `assess.v0` artifact with top-level `tool`, `version`, `policy`, `matched_rule`, `epistemic_basis`, and decision fields matching the plan/schema.
- Human output renders the same evaluated decision with concise band/risk/basis information and no extra semantics.
- Golden outputs for PROCEED, PROCEED_WITH_RISK, ESCALATE, and BLOCK are byte-stable in tests.
- Rendering uses ordered maps/structures only; no `HashMap`-driven nondeterminism is introduced.
notes: Primary files: `src/output/**`, `tests/output_schema.rs`, and any golden fixtures updated for final output bytes. Leave evaluation semantics in `src/evaluate/**`.

title: Add assess release workflow and artifact packaging
description: Add the assess release automation and packaging workflow so the repo can cut cross-platform releases like the other mature spine tools.
acceptance_criteria:
- Release workflow builds the supported target matrix, packages archives, emits checksums, provenance/SBOM/signing artifacts, and publishes a GitHub release.
- Homebrew/tap publication wiring uses the assess naming/path conventions and mirrors the proven benchmark/rvl pattern.
- Workflow uses locked Cargo/install steps compatible with the repo's CI prerequisites.
- This lane owns release automation only; it does not fill in missing product semantics.
notes: Primary files: `.github/workflows/release.yml` and any release-template metadata/files that are uniquely required by packaging. Avoid broad source edits.

title: Wire assess CLI shell, top-level command routing, and exit/refusal plumbing
description: Implement command parsing, routing, precedence, and exit/refusal plumbing so later lanes can plug real assess semantics into a stable CLI shell.
acceptance_criteria:
- `assess`, `assess witness`, `--json`, `--policy`, `--policy-id`, `--no-witness`, `--describe`, `--schema`, and `--version` parse and route according to `PLAN_ASSESS.md`.
- Exit-code mapping is explicit and tested: PROCEED=0, PROCEED_WITH_RISK/ESCALATE=1, BLOCK/refusals/parse-fatal=2.
- Structured refusal plumbing is in place for semantic failures after parse succeeds; malformed CLI usage remains on the parse/error path.
- `src/main.rs` stays thin and delegates to library/CLI modules.
notes: Primary files: `src/main.rs`, `src/lib.rs`, `src/cli/**`, plus any tiny refusal-routing glue strictly needed for command dispatch. Avoid implementing policy, bundle, or evaluator semantics here.
close_reason: Completed: CLI shell routing, exit mapping, and refusal plumbing landed

title: Implement assess witness ledger append and query surface
description: Implement the local assess witness subsystem and query subcommands as a supplemental receipt log, leaving final end-to-end run wiring to the integration lane.
acceptance_criteria:
- Successful assess runs append a local witness receipt containing tool name, input paths, policy ID/hash context, decision band, and duration unless `--no-witness` is set.
- `assess witness query`, `last`, and `count` operate over the local JSONL ledger and support the documented JSON/human surfaces.
- Witness support remains local receipt logging only; it does not become a portable artifact or alter assess decision semantics.
- Final command-path wiring of append-on-run is left to integration; this lane owns the witness subsystem and query surface.
notes: Primary files: `src/witness/**` and `tests/witness_suite.rs`. Do not take ownership of the final `run()` orchestration outside the minimal hooks needed for witness invocation.
close_reason: Implemented witness ledger append/load/query/count/last support plus witness CLI execution coverage.

title: Implement policy loading, built-in policy resolution, and validation
description: Load policies from the supported selectors, validate the narrowed v0 rule surface, and produce stable policy hashes for downstream evaluation.
acceptance_criteria:
- Policy selection resolves the v0 surface cleanly across `--policy`, `--policy-id`, builtin IDs, and configured search paths, with `E_AMBIGUOUS_POLICY` and `E_UNKNOWN_POLICY` handled deterministically.
- YAML parsing, schema validation, default-last enforcement, required `risk_code` checks, and policy SHA-256 hashing match the plan contract.
- Builtin-policy and path-based fixtures are covered in tests without leaking evaluation logic into the loader.
- This lane stops at validated policy objects and stable policy hashes; it does not choose decisions.
notes: Primary files: `src/policy/**` and `tests/policy_load.rs`. Avoid taking ownership of matcher/evaluator files.
close_reason: Completed: policy loader, resolution order, hashing, and validation landed

title: Lock assess refusal contract types
description: Lock the assess refusal-contract surface so CLI, rendering, and operator compliance all share one deterministic envelope.
acceptance_criteria:
- Refusal codes, refusal payload shapes, and any refusal-routing helpers are locked to the v0 contract and reusable by CLI/output/operator lanes.
- Contract tests cover top-level `tool = assess`, deterministic refusal payload shape, and stable exit/refusal mapping assumptions.
- This lane does not own policy/bundle schemas or evaluator semantics.
notes: Primary files: `src/refusal/**` and any tiny refusal-contract helpers that do not belong in CLI or renderer modules. Avoid `schemas/**`, `src/policy/**`, and `src/bundle/**` here.

title: Lock assess schema artifacts and shared policy/bundle types
description: Lock the assess schema artifacts and the shared policy/bundle type surface needed by policy loading, bundle parsing, evaluation, and rendering, without owning refusal handling or final rendering behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- `schemas/assess.v0.schema.json` and `schemas/policy.v0.schema.json` are locked to the v0 contract in `PLAN_ASSESS.md`.
- Shared Rust types for policy files, canonical artifact basis entries, matched-rule identity, policy hash context, and decision-band primitives compile without refusal-routing logic.
- Contract tests cover: default rule must be last, non-PROCEED rules require `risk_code`, every provided artifact remains representable in the epistemic basis, and schema-required fields stay stable.
- This lane does not own refusal envelopes, witness records, or final JSON/human rendering code.
notes: Primary files: `schemas/assess.v0.schema.json`, `schemas/policy.v0.schema.json`, `src/policy/schema.rs`, `src/bundle/artifact.rs`, and any tiny shared non-refusal enums/types. Avoid taking ownership of `src/refusal/**`, `src/witness/**`, or `src/output/**` formatting code.

title: Add compact decision summary render modes without changing assess.v0 JSON
description:
Context:
`assess` already emits one deterministic decision artifact, but in multi-tool operator workflows people still end up wrapping its JSON output just to scan the decision band, matched rule, observed tools, required tools, and refusal state across many runs. Add optional compact summary render surfaces derived from the existing decision/refusal artifact so operators can scan outcomes quickly while the canonical JSON contract stays unchanged.

Deliverables:
- add an optional compact summary render mode for decisions and refusals
- add an optional TSV summary render mode with stable columns for shell pipelines
- include at least: tool, version, outcome, decision, matched_rule, risk_code, required_tools, observed_tools, witness state when relevant, and refusal code when present
- ensure summary modes are pure renderings of the existing decision/refusal object and never perform additional policy evaluation
- update README, AGENTS, and output-focused tests to cover the new surfaces

Non-goals:
- changing rule matching semantics or policy loading
- changing the `assess.v0` JSON artifact
- teaching `assess` to interpret upstream raw metrics beyond existing exact-match policy signals

Plan alignment:
- stays inside the plan's output boundary
- preserves the 'one decision artifact' invariant for JSON mode
- improves operator ergonomics without broadening assess into a different kind of tool
acceptance_criteria:
1. `assess` can emit compact summary and TSV summary views for both decision artifacts and refusals without changing existing JSON output.
2. Summary values are rendered from the existing decision/refusal object only; no extra policy logic is introduced.
3. README, AGENTS, and output-focused tests document and verify the new surfaces.
4. The canonical JSON contract remains backward compatible.
